We need a bash command that counts subdirectories for each directory under /system, sort descending, show top 10. Use find to list directories, then for each count subdirs. Maybe use while read. Simpler: find /system -type d -print0 | while read -d '' dir; do echo "$(find "$dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | wc -l) $dir"; done | sort -nr | head -10. Use xargs? Provide one line. That's one line.
